question.text: During the dissolution period of an AIF, what document must be filed through a SEBI-registered merchant banker under Regulation 29B(2)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 29B(2) of the SEBI AIF Regulations, during the dissolution period of an AIF, an information memorandum must be filed through a SEBI-registered merchant banker. This is distinct from the placement memorandum filed at scheme launch. **The Trap:** A placement memorandum is filed at the time of launching an AIF scheme, not during dissolution. Candidates often confuse the two documents. A scheme information document relates to mutual funds, not AIFs. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests the lifecycle of an AIF from a merchant banker's perspective — from placement memorandum at launch to information memorandum at dissolution. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The dissolution-period information memorandum is often overlooked in mandate letters — include it in your scope of work and fee structure from the outset.
